title: 7 Payments Dashboard
body_text:
7	Payments Dashboard
7.1	Overview of the Payments Dashboard
The Payments Dashboard allows authorized users to apply multi-level filtering, view the results as charts or tables, compare your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs performance to anonymized peers of similar size and product usage, and export insights into multiple formats. The Payments Dashboard currently includes Interac
e-Transfer
ГӮВ®
transactions.
Central 1 typically provides up to two years of transaction history.
7.2	Filters Menu
Use the left-hand Filters menu to customize the data displayed on the Payments Dashboard. These filters affect all applicable visuals on the dashboard, whereas filters located within a specific filter only affect that visual.
Use the following filters as needed. See Figure 1.
ГўВ‘
Product Type
ГўВҖВ“ Interac e-Transfer is currently the only product available.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Measure Selection
ГўВҖВ“ Use the drop-down menu to select a measurement type. Select from Transaction Volume (default), Total Payment Amount, or Avg. Payment Amount.
ГўВ‘Вў
Date Filters ГўВҖВ“
Use the drop-down menu to select one of the pre-defined time periods (e.g., 3 months, 6 months, 9 months) or double click the calendar icons to choose start and end dates. Alternatively, a single click to the date icon(s) opens a slider that allows you to adjust the start and end dates.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Transaction Type
ГўВҖВ“ Use the drop-down menu to select a transaction type. Choose from the following options: All (default), EMT Receive, EMT Send, MR Fulfill, or MR Send.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Feature
ГўВҖВ“ Use the drop-down menu to select a feature. Choose from the following options: All (default), Account Number Routed, Auto Deposit, Money Request, Regular (Q&A), or Unknown.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Brand Name
ГўВҖВ“ Use the drop-down menu to choose a brand. This option only displays multiple options if your financial institution has more than one brand.
ГўВ‘ВҰ
Originating Channel
ГўВҖВ“ Use the drop-down menu to choose the originating channel. Choose from the following options: All (default), External API, MD Desktop, MD Mobile Web, Mobile App, Unattended, Unknown, or Version Agnostic API.
Figure 1: Filters Menu
7.3	Payments Dashboard Visuals and Common Features
The Payments Dashboard contains seven visuals that, by default, are displayed as charts. See Figure 2. This includes:
ГўВ‘
Transaction Volume
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Total Payment Amount
ГўВ‘Вў
Average Payment Amount
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Transaction Volume by [Transaction Type]
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Transaction Amount Distribution
ГўВ‘ВҘ
[Transaction Volume] Trend by [Quarter] by [Product Type]
ГўВ‘ВҰ
Cohort Comparison by [Transaction Volume]
Note:	Text surrounded by square brackets indicates that the Filters menu can be used to change the data input for the visual. The title of the visual may change when a new filter is selected.
Figure 2: Payments Dashboard Charts
The following common features are available in most visuals:
Viewing additional information
ГўВҖВ“ Hover your cursor over a specific element to view additional information. See Figure 3 for a sample.
Figure 3: Viewing Additional Information
Copying an image
ГўВҖВ“ Select the copy icon to copy an image of the visual. See Figure 4 for a sample. Follow the on-screen prompts to copy and paste the image.
Figure 4: Copying an Image
Add an alert ГўВҖВ“
Use the alert function to receive notifications on changes, trends, and other conditions. Select the bell icon, then follow the online prompts. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Adding an Alert
Accessing more options
ГўВҖВ“ Select the ellipsis icon to access additional standard options through Microsoft Power BI (e.g., Share, Add alert, Add a comment, Explore this data (preview), Export data, Show as a table, Spotlight, Get insights, Sort axis, Sort legend). See Figure 6 for a sample.
Note:	Procedures on using these standard Microsoft Power BI options are outside the scope of this document.
Figure 6: More Options
Showing the dashboard as tables
ГўВҖВ“
To view the dashboard as tables rather than charts, select the rounded rectangular icon
in the top menu bar, then select
Show visuals as tables (preview)
. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Displaying Data in Tables
7.4	Transaction Volume, Total Payment Amount, and Average Payment Amount Visuals
The Transaction Volume, Total Payment Amount, and Average Payment Amount visuals generate data from your selections in the Filters menu and donГўВҖВҷt have additional filtering or action options. A description of the visual is displayed if you hover your cursor over the info icon. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Non-Interactive Visuals
7.5	Transaction Volume by [Transaction Type] Visual
This visual displays the transaction volume based on your selection in the left-hand Filters menu. Use the Breakdown drop-down menu to view the transaction volume by transaction type (default), feature, or originating channel. Once selected, the title of the visual updates to reflect your selection (e.g., selecting ГўВҖВңFeatureГўВҖВқ in the drop-down menu changes the title to Transaction Volume by [Feature]). See Figure 9 and Figure 10. Filters selected from the drop-down menu only affect this visual, not the whole dashboard.
Figure 9: Transaction Volume by [Transaction Type] ГўВҖВ“ Default View
Figure 10: Breakdown Menu
7.6	Transaction Amount Distribution Visual
This visual displays the distribution of transactions by amount range based on your selection in the left-hand Filters menu. The default setting shows content in $10 increments. Use the Bin Size drop-down menu to change the amount range displayed in each increment (e.g., selecting $100 shows bars in $100 dollar increments). See Figure 11 and Figure 12.
Figure 11: Transaction Amount Distribution ГўВҖВ“ Default View
Figure 12: Transaction Amount Distribution Bin Size Menu
7.7 	[Transaction Volume] Trend by [Quarter] by [Product Type] Visual
This visual displays the trend (transaction volume, total payment amount, or average payment amount) by time period (month, quarter, or year) by an additional filter (product type, transaction type, feature, or originating channel). The default filters are ГўВҖВңtransaction volumeГўВҖВқ by ГўВҖВңquarterГўВҖВқ by ГўВҖВңproduct typeГўВҖВқ. While the visual is based on your selection in the left-hand Filters menu, there are also filters available from drop-down menus inside the visual. Filters selected from the drop-down menus only affect this visual, not the whole dashboard. See Figure 13, Figure 14, and Figure 15.
Figure 13: [Transaction Volume] Trend by [Quarter] by [Product Type] ГўВҖВ“ Default View
Figure 14: Time Period Menu
Figure 15: Breakdown Menu
To turn the data labels on or off, select the Data Labels toggle switch. See Figure 16. This figure shows the data labels off.
Figure 16: Data Labels Toggle Switch
7.8	Cohort Comparison by [Transaction Volume] Visual
This visual compares your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs data with anonymized peers of similar size and product usage. The displayed data is based on your selection in the left-hand Filters menu. A description of the visual is displayed if you hover your cursor over the info icon. See Figure 17.
Figure 17: Cohort Comparison by [Transaction Volume]
END
